details.title: Portfolio
details.content:
A minimal, interaction-focused developer portfolio.

Built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, this portfolio features a clean design and smooth animations. It showcases my projects, work experience, and achievements in an engaging way.

I took inspiration from games like Dead Space 2 and Tom Clancy’s The Division. I’m really into microinteractions—small, subtle details that make interfaces feel alive without being distracting.

details.title: Kenvue
details.content:
interned at kenvue solutions india - bangalore

I joined Kenvue Solutions India as an intern, initially starting with the frontend since it was a good entry point. Over time, as I got more comfortable and earned trust within the team, I gradually took on backend work as well.

Most of my work went into building an observability platform that streams Kafka logs and helps teams monitor the health and performance of their services. It was something that other engineers would rely on day-to-day, which made the work feel meaningful.

A big chunk of my time was spent on optimization. A lot of the existing code wasn’t very efficient, so I worked on improving performance and making things more reliable. It wasn’t always straightforward, but I learned a lot by digging through and refining what was already there.

I also built parts of the UI using Next.js and SWR, and worked on backend services with FastAPI and PostgreSQL. On the side, I contributed to improving code quality with SonarQube and helped automate workflows using Jenkins.

Overall, it was a structured corporate environment, but my team made a huge difference. They were supportive, gave me room to grow, and made the whole experience genuinely enjoyable.

title: won runner up at tri-nit hack
